rulings.Case Title: ALBERTO PEDRUCO VS. JENNIFER MATTHEWS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication, Of Plaintiff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 24 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT JENNIFER MATHEWS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication, Of Plaintiff'S Complaint. The motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant fails to show that plaintiff cannot establish his libel cause of action. This case does not involve a public concern. Rather, the thrust of plaintiff's complaint to the human resources department related to personal items missing from her locker. Plaintiff does not need to prove malice. The substance of defendant's e-mail, e.g., that plaintiff was under investigation for gender discrimination, was false. (Fernandez Depo., 30:6-21). The statements were libel on their face and tended to injure plaintiff in his occupation. Therefore, plaintiff need not prove special damages. See Cal. Civil Code secs. 45 and 45a; Walker v. Kiousis (2001) 93 Cal.App.4th 1432, 1441-1442. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE HARRIS VS. DISCOUNT BEAUTY SUPPLY AKA BEAUTY SUPPLY WARE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Two (2) Discovery Orders And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 24, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT BEAUTY SUPPLY WAREHOUSE'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Two (2) Discovery Orders And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO TENTATIVE RULING. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO APPEAR AT THE 9:00 AM HEARING PREPARED TO ARGUE THE MOTION. NO COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOTION PAPERS OR OPPOSITION WERE FILED WITH THE COURT; HOWEVER, THE COURT HAS OBTAINED COPIES OF THE PAPERS THIS MORNING AND NEEDS FURTHER CLARIFICATION FROM THE PARTIES. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBIN WILSON VS. JUDITH A BOLTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 24 2015, Line 9, Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint. Denied. Defendant Corey Bolter generally appeared in the case by filing a declaration in support of the opposition to the issuance of the preliminary injunction. (Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. A.) "The filing of an affidavit, other than for the purpose of presenting matters bearing on the court's jurisdiction, constitutes a general appearance." (Wilson v. Barry (1951) 102 Cal.App.2d 778, 781.) Alternatively, Plaintiff's evidence establishes that Thompson Welch Soroko Gilbert LLP ("TWSG") represented Defendant Corey Bolter at the time that TWSG executed a stipulation on his behalf. (Declaration of Nelson Goodell.) The Court finds this evidence to be more credible than Defendant Corey Bolter's self-serving declaration that TWSG was not authorized "to accept service or sign an order on my behalf in any way." (Declaration of Corey Bolter, par. 6.) Entering into a stipulation also constitutes a general appearance. (See RCA Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (1975) 47 Cal.App.3d 1007, 1010.) Defendant Corey Bolter shall answer the complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROBIN WILSON VS. JUDITH A BOLTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause And Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 24 2015, Line 9, Order To Show Cause And Temporary Restraining Order. Granted. Plaintiff has established a likelihood of prevailing on the merits and has shown that she will be irreparably harmed if the preliminary injunction is not issued. The Court did not consider Defendant's opposition in ruling on this motion. Defendant failed to provide a courtesy copy of the opposition papers in violation of Local Rule 2.6B and Department 501's Order dated January 30, 2015. As to the undertaking requirement, Plaintiff shall continue to make monthly payments of $3,500, as set forth in Plaintiff's proposed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
